title: Search and return match data from all CSV files
evidence: data_loader.py:39-78 defines load functions for all 5 match datasets (Brasileirao, Copa do Brasil, Libertadores, BR-Football, Historical); server.py:27-51 implements search_matches tool

title: Search and return player data
evidence: data_loader.py:130+ loads FIFA player data; server.py:96-120 implements search_players tool with filters for name, nationality, club, position, overall rating

title: Calculate basic statistics (wins, losses, goals)
evidence: data_loader.py:200+ implements team_statistics and match_statistics methods; server.py:55-76 and 143-156 expose these as tools

title: Compare teams head-to-head
evidence: data_loader.py:270+ implements head_to_head method returning wins/losses/draws; server.py:78-92 implements get_head_to_head tool

title: Handle team name variations correctly
evidence: data_loader.py:8-22 implements _normalize_team_name function handling state suffixes (-SP, -RJ, etc.); tests/test_data_loader.py:15-24 validates normalization

title: Return properly formatted responses
evidence: server.py:9-23 implements _df_to_str formatting; server.py uses JSON output for statistics; tests validate output format

title: Simple lookups respond in < 2 seconds
evidence: Test execution completed in 7.69s for 53 tests including data loading and querying; individual lookup tests pass
suggestion: Requirement likely implemented but not formally performance tested

title: Aggregate queries respond in < 5 seconds
evidence: Test execution completed in 7.69s for 53 tests; aggregate stats tests pass indicating reasonable performance
suggestion: Requirement likely implemented but not formally performance tested

title: No timeout errors
evidence: All 53 tests completed without timeout; no timeout handling code needed

title: All 6 CSV files are loadable and queryable
evidence: data_loader.py implements loaders for: Brasileirao (line 39), Copa do Brasil (53), Libertadores (67), BR-Football (81), Historical (100+), FIFA (130+); tests pass for all

title: At least 20 sample questions can be answered
evidence: Server implements 6 tools (search_matches, get_team_statistics, get_head_to_head, search_players, get_competition_standings, get_match_statistics) supporting multiple question types; tests validate specific queries
suggestion: Code appears capable of answering 20+ sample questions but no formal validation of 20 distinct question examples

title: Cross-file queries work
evidence: data_loader.py:163+ creates _all_matches combining all 5 match datasets; search_matches searches across all combined matches

title: Import block is un-sorted or un-formatted
evidence: data_loader.py:1-3 has unsorted imports (os, pandas, pathlib)
suggestion: Use `ruff check --fix` or manually organize imports

title: Unused import: os
evidence: data_loader.py:1 imports os but never uses it

title: Line too long (multiple instances)
evidence: data_loader.py:50, 64, 78, 92, 116, 163, 169 exceed 88-char limit (44 total lint errors found)
suggestion: Run `ruff check --fix` to auto-fix 17 fixable errors
